dataset_classification.reason: The dev/calibration/sealed questions and labels are generated by scripts/generate_phase5_questions_labels.py from hardcoded COMPANY_DATA embedded in the repository. Any repository accessor can regenerate the golden labels, so this dataset does NOT provide blind generalization evidence. It is a deterministic synthetic held-out regression set suitable for testing the evaluation pipeline, metric implementation, regression behavior, error injection, and ablation wiring — but NOT for proving real blind sealed generalization.
intended_use: ablation wiring verification (feature flags reach runtime)
not_intended_for: production quality gating based on absolute pass rates
dataset_classification.true_sealed_v3_note: A non-reconstructible Sealed v3 set would require an external custodian who generates and holds labels outside the repository. It is out of scope for Phase 5 v2 closure.

stage_1_replay.description: Apply params via apply_params_to_prediction() on existing calibration predictions. Fast, no RAG engine required.

stage_2_end_to_end_rerun.description: Run RAG engine with winner config from Stage 1 to verify replay matches reality.

two_stage_calibration.safe_zero_rule: When safe_candidates == 0, select baseline (never select from unsafe candidates)

calibration_search_space_note: All params in calibration_search_space must be truly applicable at runtime via src/evaluation/engine_factory.py:apply_calibration_params_runtime(). The previous sufficiency_best_score_threshold, sufficiency_average_score_threshold, and document_coverage_threshold were removed because they do not exist as runtime attributes on EvidenceSufficiencyEvaluator or any engine component. They are replaced by rrf_sufficiency_threshold and dense_sufficiency_threshold, which patch engine._sufficiency_evaluator._rrf_threshold and _dense_threshold respectively. The runtime branching logic (best_score < 0.05 -> rrf_threshold, else dense_threshold) is mirrored in calibration.py:apply_params_to_prediction().

steps: eliminate candidates with any safety_release_metric worse than baseline
eliminate candidates with invalid_citation_release higher than baseline
eliminate candidates with unsafe_content_release higher than baseline
eliminate candidates with system_error_rate worse than baseline
maximize macro_strict_pass_rate among remaining safe candidates
false_block_rate is a utility metric, NOT a safety gate
candidate_selection_rule.safe_zero_rule: when safe_candidates == 0, return baseline (never select from unsafe)

checks: git HEAD must match the RC-frozen expected_commit
git worktree must be clean (no uncommitted changes)
selected-config.json must exist and be loadable
sealed questions SHA256 must match the value frozen at RC time
evaluation protocol SHA256 must match the value frozen at RC time

sentinel_query_verification.rationale: A zero-result sentinel query indicates an index wiring failure (partition index not built, user_id mismatch, embedding model mismatch, or BM25 path error), NOT a real system capability result. The run must abort before producing held-out predictions.

calibration_param_injection.rationale: The winner config from calibration Stage 1 must be truly injected into the RAGEngine at runtime, not just recorded in a JSON file. Stage 2 parity diff is only meaningful if the params actually reach the engine components.

feature_flag_injection.rationale: Each ablation variant A0-A9 must actually toggle the targeted component at runtime. Flags that are declared but not wired produce identical engine behavior to A0, making ablation deltas meaningless.

sealed_run_policy_superseded_note: The previous sealed_run_policy field is superseded by held_out_run_policy. The dataset is classified as synthetic_held_out (see dataset_classification), not a true sealed evaluation.
